business.headline: Foreign investors get major capital gains tax relief from the governmentThe Centre is weighing tax changes to make Indian government bonds more attractive to foreign investors. The proposed steps are aimed at boosting inflows, supporting the rupee and easing market pressure.

Why file your ITR before July 31? These 3 benefits could helpWith the July 31 ITR deadline approaching, taxpayers have another reason to avoid delays. Filing your return on time can unlock several benefits that could prove useful throughout the year.

India responds to proposed US tariffs as trade pact negotiations continueWhile discussions over the tariff proposal continue, India and the United States are also working to finalise a broader trade agreement.

CCPA fines PhysicsWallah and McAfee for using dark patterns on digital platformsCCPA has also directed both companies to remove dark pattern practices that influenced consumer choices on their digital platforms.

Only 1% left to resolve in India- US trade pact, says US envoy Sergio GorSpeaking at Citi's 2026 India Conference on Wednesday, Gor said both countries were working to resolve the remaining "one per cent" sticking point and were hopeful of completing the agreement within the next several weeks.

Sensex ends 304 points lower as crude nears $100, IT stocks crackThe S&P BSE Sensex ended 303.67 points lower, or 0.41%, at 74,346.17, while the NSE Nifty50 fell 77.95 points, or 0.33%, to close at 23,405.60.

Big relief for airlines as govt caps jet fuel prices, clears Rs 10,000-crore fundThe decision comes amid recent developments in West Asia that have pushed up international ATF prices and increased pressure on airlines.

RBI MPC meeting starts today: Will rising inflation risks lead to a rate hike?The RBI's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) begins its three-day meeting today amid rising concerns over inflation, higher crude oil prices and global uncertainties. Will these factors be enough to trigger a rate hike?

Not correct: RBI rejects $12-billion gold sale report, says reserves unchangedIn a clarification issued on Tuesday, the RBI shared reports suggesting it had sold part of its gold holdings were "not correct" and highlighted that its physical stock of gold remains unchanged at 880.52 tonnes.

Want to use EPFO's UPI feature? Fix these 5 things firstImagine accessing your PF savings as easily as making a UPI payment. That's the experience EPFO is aiming to offer soon. However, the new feature will work smoothly only if your account details are accurate. Experts recommend checking five key areas now to avoid problems later.

Gold, silver prices are down again. Should investors buy, hold or wait?If you've been tracking gold and silver prices recently, you've probably noticed that the rally has lost some steam. Both metals have slipped despite ongoing geopolitical uncertainty, leaving investors with an important decision to make: buy more, stay invested, or wait for the dust to settle.

TCS, Tech Mahindra, HCLTech crash up to 8%: Why are IT stocks falling todayIT stocks fell sharply on Wednesday as investors booked profits after a two-day rally. Weak ADR cues, cooling global software momentum and uncertainty over AI gains weighed on sentiment.

US proposes up to 12.5% tariff on India, China over forced labour concernsThe proposed action, announced by the Office of the United States Trade Representative (USTR), comes under Section 301 of the US Trade Act of 1974, the same law that was used to impose tariffs on Chinese imports during President Donald Trump's first term.

Sensex crashes 700 points. What's behind today's market meltdown?Markets opened sharply lower as renewed Iran-US tensions, higher crude prices and heavy IT profit-booking hit trade on Wednesday. The weaker rupee, persistent FPI outflow concerns and the RBI policy outlook kept volatility elevated.

Sensex, Nifty tank in early trade over crude oil price rise; TCS falls 6%The S&P BSE Sensex fell 763.45 points, or 1.02%, to 73,886.39 in early trade, while the NSE Nifty50 declined 213.75 points, or 0.91%, to 23,269.80.

IndusInd Bank faces fresh whistleblower allegations; complaint sent to PMO, RBIA whistleblower complaint has sought a probe into alleged insider trading and governance lapses at IndusInd Bank. The allegations add fresh scrutiny as the lender remains under watch over its Rs 2,000-crore derivatives accounting discrepancy.

Not just the AI king. Nvidia may be the world's safest companyNvidia's credit default swap has traded close to US sovereign levels as its market value and earnings surge. The reading reflects unusually strong confidence in the AI chipmaker's ability to withstand shocks and meet obligations.

US wants to end Russian oil sanctions waiver: How it will hit IndiaThe US has repeatedly criticised purchases of Russian oil since the Ukraine conflict began, claiming that revenues from crude exports help fund Moscow's military campaign.

Maharashtra govt buys Mumbai's landmark Air India building for Rs 1,601 croreThe state government plans to shift a number of its offices to the Air India building, many of which are currently functioning from rented premises across Mumbai.

Oyo parent Prism gets Sebi nod for Rs 6,650 crore IPO, targets up to $8 billion valuationFollowing Sebi's approval, the company is now expected to file an Updated Draft Red Herring Prospectus (UDRHP-1), which will be made available for public review and comments.

entertainment.headline: Peddi X reviews: Fans crown Ram Charan 'king', praise his brilliant performance Ram Charan's Peddi has sparked a wave of reactions on X after its theatrical release on June 5, with fans praising the actor's performance and calling it one of the finest of his career.

NGO seeks action against Shilpa Shinde over 'false' harassment claim Actor Shilpa Shinde has sparked controversy after admitting that her sexual harassment complaint against Bhabiji Ghar Par Hain's producer was false. Men's rights organisation to seek police action and triggered reactions from several members of the entertainment industry.

I attended a film press conference where the press never got to speak If journalists can't ask questions, is it really still called a press conference? Across film promotions, carefully curated interactions are replacing genuine conversations, with stars and PR teams controlling the narrative at every step. The result: plenty of publicity, but very little press.

Upasana joins fans, showers confetti at Ram Charan's Peddi screening. Video Upasana Kamineni Konidela was swept up in the Peddi fever as she watched the film with fans at a Hyderabad theatre. Videos show her cheering for Ram Charan and joining the confetti-filled celebrations inside the auditorium.

Peddi review: Ram Charan and the emotions hit sixes, the writing gets run outRelease Date:Thu Jun 04, 2026 Peddi movie review: Ram Charan's Peddi follows a nameless village's fight for a railway station and identity. The film lands emotionally despite visible logic gaps, weak romance writing and uneven editing.

Supergirl final trailer: Milly Alcock defies Superman; Jason Momoa joins forces The final trailer for Supergirl shows Kara Zor-El teaming with Ruthye while clashing with Superman's wider legacy. It also brings Jason Momoa's Lobo into the DC Universe and expands the story of Krypton and the House of El.

Is Allu Arjun-Lokesh Kanagaraj's AA23 shelved? Writer Rathna Kumar clarifies Writer Rathna Kumar shared a social media post about Allu Arjun-Lokesh Kanagaraj's AA23 amid speculations about the film getting shelved.

Peddi fever begins: Ram Charan fans burst crackers at preview shows. Video Ram Charan's Peddi drew enthusiastic fans to special preview shows at Bengaluru's Brunda theatre. The turnout gave the screenings a festive mood ahead of the film's release.

How Obsession's most disturbing scene was shot using a doll and jelly-glass A behind-the-scenes footage shows how Obsession staged its most disturbing attack with practical effects. The making of the scene highlights how a doll, fake blood and jelly-like glass created its impact.

When Aamir Khan said that marriage may not suit him at 60 Aamir Khan is set to formalise his relationship with Gauri Spratt in an intimate registered marriage. The development has revived his earlier remark that getting married at 60 would not suit him.

Mukesh Ambani impressed by Dhurandhar's Rs 3,000 crore: 40% of India's box office Mukesh Ambani praised the Dhurandhar franchise for its unprecedented box office success, contributing 40% of India's total collections and setting new industry benchmarks.

Peddi advance booking: Ram Charan's sports drama earns Rs 35 crore globally Ram Charan's Peddi has grossed Rs 35 crore worldwide in advance sales before its June 4 release. The early trend highlights strong Telugu market support as the film faces a key test beyond its core base.

Anushka Sharma says homeopathy helped her, sparks age-old medicine debate Anushka Sharma shared a video of Dr Rajan Sankaran and said homeopathy shaped her health journey. The post split social media, with critics questioning celebrity influence and supporters calling it personal choice.

Long-lost friends to life partners: Timeline of Aamir Khan-Gauri Spratt relationship Aamir Khan and Gauri Spratt are set to formalise their relationship through a registered marriage at his home on July 5. The private ceremony follows a year of widely-discussed public appearances.

RGV owes technicians Rs 1 crore: Ashoke Pandit slams director's Don 3 row remarks Ashoke Pandit launched a scathing attack on Ram Gopal Varma, demanding an unconditional apology for his recent controversial social media posts on the Don 3 dispute.

The Gupt confession: Why Bobby Deol refused an intimate scene with Manisha Koirala Bobby Deol recalled refusing to shoot an intimate scene with Manisha Koirala during a song sequence in Gupt. He also revisited a failed prank from the sets of the 1997 film.

Ram Charan fan club trolled for idol that doesn't resemble him, apologises Ram Charan fans in Telangana's Kukatpally unveiled a Peddi-inspired idol at Arjun Theatre that drew mockery online for not resembling the actor. The backlash prompted an apology from the fan club over the misfire.

Aamir Khan, 61, to marry Gauri Spratt on July 5; SRK and Salman may attend Aamir Khan and Gauri Spratt will formalise their relationship in a private registered marriage at his home on July 5. The ceremony reflects the couple's decision to begin this new chapter quietly with their families and close friends.

Dinesh Vijan jokes Shahid Kapoor's love life was a hexagon. Actor responds At the Cocktail 2 trailer launch, Dinesh Vijan joked that Shahid Kapoor's love life was a hexagon, not a triangle. This invited another candid remark from the actor, who referenced that it was rather a circle.

general news.headline: Never got NOC, fled in fear after fire erupted: Delhi hotel owner admits to copsThe investigation into the devastating fire at Delhi's Malviya Nagar that claimed 21 lives has revealed fresh details about the actions and claims of Bajaj, who was arrested by Delhi Police in connection with the tragedy.

Foreign investors get major capital gains tax relief from the governmentThe Centre is weighing tax changes to make Indian government bonds more attractive to foreign investors. The proposed steps are aimed at boosting inflows, supporting the rupee and easing market pressure.

US House backs resolution to end Iran war, 3 Republicans give supportThe House approved a war powers resolution to block Donald Trump from continuing the Iran conflict, with four Republicans joining Democrats. The mostly symbolic vote signalled deeper Republican unease and added to recent congressional pushback against him.

The BJP wouldn't want the TMC to collapse completely. Here's whyThe TMC's internal turmoil has delighted many in the BJP. But others in the party worry that if the Mamata Banerjee-founded party collapses entirely, the BJP might end up facing the rapidly reviving CPI(M)-led Left Front. Then there could be the Left-Congress combine too.

Peddi review: Ram Charan and the emotions hit sixes, the writing gets run outRelease Date:Thu Jun 04, 2026Peddi movie review: Ram Charan's Peddi follows a nameless village's fight for a railway station and identity. The film lands emotionally despite visible logic gaps, weak romance writing and uneven editing.

A Gurugram family visited Delhi for their ailing father. Hotel blaze claimed 8 livesEight members of a Gurugram family died in South Delhi's Malviya Nagar hotel fire while staying near Max Hospital for an ailing relative. They had checked into Flourish Stay in order to stay near the hospital where the family patriarch, Radheshayam Aggarwal, was undergoing treatment.

He is sixteen. He broke into NEET and JEE to fix itAt just 16, Dubai-based CBSE student Rylen Anil has exposed vulnerabilities in both the NEET and JEE Advanced systems within days of each other. In an interview with India Today, he spoke about reporting the flaws, receiving a response from top officials, and why he believes young ethical hackers are helping make India's education systems more secure.

Viral proposal, possible punishment: Army officer faces heat over romantic gestureAfter graduating in Nashik, Capt Bharat Bhardwaj proposed to his fiancée beside a helicopter at the ceremony venue. The viral moment, though widely praised online, is now under scrutiny over military protocol and decorum.

Are Gambhir and selectors on the same page over India's new T20I captain choice?Gautam Gambhir and the selectors are aligned on moving past Suryakumar Yadav, but not on who should lead India's T20I side next. Gambhir seems to have doubts about Shreyas Iyer as the new captain and has his own preferred choice for the role.

Pak men who gang-raped French tourist in front of her children to be executedA French woman was inside the vehicle with the doors locked after it ran out of fuel. The attackers smashed a window, dragged her out, and raped her at gunpoint in front of her children.

Hidden in Karnataka Cabinet, the all-powerful SiddaramaiahDK Shivakumar has become the Karnataka CM after years of posturing against his former boss and Congress veteran, Siddaramaiah. However, even as Shivakumar is the CM, it's seemingly former CM Siddaramaiah who's holding all the cards.

Shooting in more moderate manner: Trump's new definition of truce amid conflictDonald Trump described the US-Iran ceasefire as firing in a more moderate manner during an Oval Office interaction. His remarks came amid fresh attacks, but he said the situation was under control and talks were continuing.

I am a paediatrician. 5 things I never do when my child is sickWhen a child falls sick, panic can lead parents to make decisions that may do more harm than good. A pediatrician shares the five things she never does and the science-backed approach she follows instead.

Can't do it again: NEET aspirant dies by suicide after depression over paper leakA NEET aspirant from Madhya Pradesh died by suicide after the exam she took was cancelled amid the paper leak row. Her family said the uncertainty over the re-test deepened her distress despite months of preparation and financial sacrifice.

Tarique Rahman's first stop isn't India or China. Why he chose MalaysiaBangladesh PM Tarique Rahman will reportedly visit Malaysia on June 21. It would be his first foreign trip. Why didn't he choose India or China?

Beauty pageant under scanner: CCI orders investigation into Mrs India IncCCI has ordered a detailed investigation into Mrs India Inc over a former contestant's complaint. The order flags allegedly onerous clauses, undisclosed costs and curbs on participants' professional choices.

Tourists doing garba at landslide zone sparks debate on sensitivity and civic senseA video of tourists dancing Garba near a landslide-hit road has sparked criticism online. The clip has widened a debate on travel etiquette, civic sense and social media behaviour.

Suryakumar to be sacked, Shreyas Iyer in 3-way race for India's T20I captaincyIs this the end of SKY era? Shreyas Iyer is the front-runner to become India's new T20I skipper for the Ireland and England series. The move would indicate a long-term leadership reset, with Ishan Kishan and Tilak Varma also in contention.

Madhya Pradesh man hits wife during fight, she retaliates and beats him to deathPolice said Deendayal Kushwaha had visited his wife's parents' home, where she had been residing for several years. During the visit, a dispute allegedly erupted between the couple and quickly turned violent.

Sam Altman says rising AI cost is a huge issue, but he is not sure whyOpenAI CEO Sam Altman has claimed that while companies are now increasingly concerned about rising AI costs, just a few months ago, it was not really an issue. Altman's comments come at a time when companies like Uber, Amazon, and Microsoft are rethinking how employees use AI tools due to higher token usage.

Flex-fuel results in a drop in mileage, but you can save if…The next big trend in India is flex-fuel where there is a guaranteed drop in fuel economy. But can it save you money? We try and answer the question.

Free travel, jobs and youth clubs: DK Shivakumar's first big announcementsHours after taking oath, DK Shivakumar announced free student bus passes, a jobs exchange and youth clubs in his first Cabinet meeting. The package set the tone for his government's focus on youth welfare, employment and urban infrastructure.

Why is it raining so heavily in Mumbai without the monsoon?Mumbai witnessed rain and winds just days before the expected onset of southwest monsoon over Kerala, which would eventually signal the beginning of India's rainy season.

4 patients dead, over 20 rescued after massive fire at Bihar hospitalSeveral critically ill patients have been shifted to nearby hospitals, and authorities are currently verifying their condition and whereabouts. The Civil Surgeon has also reached the spot to oversee the situation and coordinate medical assistance.

sports.headline: Indonesia Open: Familiar foe An Se Young proves too strong for PV Sindhu yet again PV Sindhu's Indonesia Open campaign ended in the second round as world No. 1 An Se Young claimed a 21-17, 21-15 victory, extending her perfect head-to-head record against the Indian to 10-0.

Are Gambhir and selectors on the same page over India's new T20I captain choice? Gautam Gambhir and the selectors are aligned on moving past Suryakumar Yadav, but not on who should lead India's T20I side next. Gambhir seems to have doubts about Shreyas Iyer as the new captain and has his own preferred choice for the role.

Indonesia Open Live Score: PV Sindhu loses again to An Se Young in 2nd round PV Sindhu vs An Se Young live score, Indonesia Open: An Se Young extended her dominance over PV Sindhu with a 21-17, 21-15 victory. The Korean claimed her 10th straight win over the Indian, sealing the match in just two games.

What's wrong with Smriti Mandhana? India need her firing for T20 World Cup Women's T20 World Cup 2026: Smriti Mandhana has been well below her best in T20Is this year, and after another disappointing series against England, India will be eager for their star batter to rediscover her form and rhythm at the earliest.

ENG vs NZ Tests: Full schedule, squads, livestreaming and all you need to know England kick off their home summer with a three-Test series against New Zealand, beginning at Lord's on June 4. Part of the ICC World Test Championship 2025-27 cycle, the series offers both sides a valuable chance to boost their standing, with England looking to rebound from a disappointing Ashes campaign and New Zealand aiming to build on their strong recent form.

French Open: Devastated Matteo Berrettini opens up after quarterfinal heartbreak Matteo Berrettini retired hurt during his Roland Garros quarterfinal against Matteo Arnaldi after suffering hip pain. The fresh setback ended his comeback run in Paris and left him speaking of his frustration with repeated retirements.

Pakistan Women face reality check before World Cup with Ireland series shocker Pakistan Women endured a disappointing Ireland tri-series campaign, failing to reach the final after a washout against West Indies on Wednesday. They will now shift focus to preparations for the Women's T20 World Cup 2026 with two warm-up matches in Derby.

Arnav Paparkar's dream run ends with third round defeat in French Open boys singles French Open 2026: Arnav Paparkar's promising Roland Garros 2026 Boys Singles run ended in the third round, as he went down 7-5, 6-2 to fourth seed Jack Kennedy in the Round of 16.

Flavio Cobolli fights past Felix Auger-Aliassime to reach maiden Grand Slam semis French Open 2026: Flavio Cobolli delivered the defining win of his career, rallying past Felix Auger-Aliassime 4-6, 6-4, 6-4, 6-4 at Roland Garros to reach his maiden Grand Slam semifinal.

FIFA World Cup: Switzerland's Embolo awaits US clearance after visa submission Switzerland forward Breel Embolo applied for an emergency U.S. visa at the American embassy in Bern on Wednesday, a day after he was denied boarding the national team's World Cup-bound flight because of a criminal conviction that recently became legally binding.

Praggnanandhaa relishes having a shot at title after ending Carlsen hopes Fresh from his second win over Magnus Carlsen, R Praggnanandhaa said he was happy to remain in the title race as Norway Chess enters its final two rounds after the rest day, with the Indian Grandmaster relishing his chance to fight for the crown.

Want to quit tennis right now: Sabalenka drops a bomb after stunning exitRoland Garros: Aryna Sabalenka said she wanted to quit tennis after blowing a set-and-break lead to Diana Shnaider in the French Open quarter-final. Her remarks laid bare the mental strain of repeated setbacks on clay and grass.

Sooryavanshi is a special talent, should not change anything: Virat Kohli's coachSuperstar Virat Kohli's childhood coach, Rajkumar Sharma, has described teenage batting sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi as the "rarest of the rare" talent in T20 cricket, urging the youngster to stay true to his fearless approach and avoid making any changes to his game.

MPL helping state talent turn IPL dreams into reality: MPCA PresidentMadhya Pradesh Cricket Association (MPCA) President Mahanaaryaman Scindia said the addition of new franchises in MPL has opened up more doors for young cricketers across the state, giving them a larger platform to showcase their skills and accelerate their cricketing journey.

Mandhana inspires me, Richa Ghosh always supports me: Bengal's Ankita ChakrabortyAnkita Chakraborty said Smriti Mandhana inspires her while Richa Ghosh remains a trusted source of support ahead of the Bengal T20 League. The Adamas Howrah Warriors captain also highlighted how family values, Bengal's cricket structure and team balance shape her plans for the season.

World No.1 Aryna Sabalenka stunned as Diana Shnaider pulls off huge upset French Open Live Updates Diana Shnaider scripted one of the biggest upsets of Roland Garros 2026, rallying from 3-6, 3-5 down to stun World No. 1 Aryna Sabalenka 3-6, 7-5, 6-0 and reach her maiden Grand Slam semi-final.

Suryakumar to be sacked, Shreyas Iyer in 3-way race for India's T20I captaincyIs this the end of SKY era? Shreyas Iyer is the front-runner to become India's new T20I skipper for the Ireland and England series. The move would indicate a long-term leadership reset, with Ishan Kishan and Tilak Varma also in contention.

Indian national anthem in Greece: Atiqa Mir races to most dominant win of her careerAtiqa Mir swept qualifying, heats and finals in Greece to seal her most dominant international karting win. The Indian anthem played on the podium as the 11-year-old underlined her world-stage promise.

Archer loves playing for England: Stokes defends pacer's IPL-enforced absenceEngland captain Ben Stokes has thrown his support behind Jofra Archer, saying the fast bowler remains fully committed to England despite missing the first Test against New Zealand due to his IPL commitments.

Mohammed Siraj bowls full tilt on hot day in Chandigarh, eases concerns over injuryIndia vs Afghanistan: Mohammed Siraj bowled at full tilt in oppressive New Chandigarh heat, easing fitness worries before India's Afghanistan Test. His spell strengthens India's plans with Jasprit Bumrah absent and selection calls still taking shape.

Group F preview: Samurai speed, total football, styles clash awaitsFIFA World Cup 2026, Group F Preview: The Oranje's Total Football meets Japan's high-speed press, Sweden's lethal attack and Tunisia looking to cause havoc. Group F is a tactical masterclass where the top dogs must deliver.

No Iga Swiatek, but Maja Chwalinska keeps Polish flag flying with dream runFrench Open 2026: With Iga Swiatek out of the tournament, qualifier Maja Chwalinska has emerged as Poland's unlikely new hope, reaching the Roland Garros semi-finals with a stunning win over Anna Kalinskaya.

Ayush Shetty stages sensational comeback win, Prannoy crashes outAyush Shetty recovered from a poor start to beat Weng Hongyang and reach the last 16. The win gave India some relief after Satwik-Chirag's injury exit, even as HS Prannoy fell short.

Ek Bihari sab pe bhaari: RCB finally reveal Virat Kohli's chat with SooryavanshiAfter Royal Challengers Bengaluru's IPL final win, Virat Kohli sought out Vaibhav Sooryavanshi for a brief but telling exchange. Vaibhav Sooryavanshi was in Ahmedabad on the day of the final after having swept the awards for his extraordinary season.

Archer has no interest in Tests: Simon Doull slams pacer's England absence for IPLFormer New Zealand pacer Simon Doull has criticised Jofra Archer's absence from England's squad for the first Test against New Zealand, questioning the pacer's commitment to the longest format after he remained with Rajasthan Royals for the IPL playoffs.

Chasing immortality: The legendary veterans eyeing a final FIFA World CupFIFA World Cup 2026: The countdown is on for the 2026 FIFA World Cup, where a golden generation of veterans faces its final stand. From Luka Modrić to Mohamed Salah, these ageing masters must defy Father Time and brutal summer heat for one last shot at glory.

Satwik's injury halts Sat-Chi at Indonesia Open days after ending title droughtSatwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty retired midway through their Indonesia Open opener with Satwik clutching his right shoulder. The setback came two days after their Singapore Open title, shifting focus to recovery before major events.

French Open Highlights: Cobolli eliminates Auger-Aliassime, Sabalenka outFrench Open 2026 Day 11 Highlights: Felix Auger-Aliassime has been knocked out after Flavio Cobolli defeated him in the quarters of the men's singles. Earlier, Diana Shnaider knocked World No.1 Aryna Sabalenka in the quarterfinals. Maja Chwalinska also knocked out Anna Kalinskaya. The qualifier beat the seeded Russian in straight sets.

F1 2026: Charles Leclerc extends stay with Ferrari, signs multi-year extensionFerrari has tied Charles Leclerc down to a new multi-year deal ahead of his home race in Monaco. Leclerc has been the best driver for Ferrari this season and is challenging Mercedes' Kimi Antonelli and George Russell.

Did Vaibhav Sooryavanshi insult Harsha Bhogle? Commentator calls out viral clipHarsha Bhogle has dismissed a viral video claiming Vaibhav Sooryavanshi insulted Virat Kohli. The clip depicts Sooryavanshi as brash and arrogant but Bhogle called out the creators for their attempt to tarnish the young prodigy's reputation.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
